scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT RELATING TO IMMOVABLE PROPERTY WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty-Five Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date fixed for performance, or if no date is fixed, when the Plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. That on 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell regarding the immovable property described hereinbelow, whereby the Defendant agreed to sell and the Plaintiff agreed to purchase the said property for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The said Agreement to Sell is original and is marked as Annexure A.

6. That the subject matter of the Agreement is a residential flat bearing No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, more particularly described in the Schedule of Property hereinbelow, bearing Survey Number {{PROPERTY_SURVEY_NUMBER}}.

7. That pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 itself via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654. The proof of payment is marked as Annexure B.

8. That as per the covenants of the Agreement, the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the Sale Deed.

9. That the Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the obligations under the Agreement, including the payment of the balance consideration within the stipulated time.

10. That despite the Plaintiff's readiness, the Defendant vide letter dated 05.06.2024 expressly refused to execute the Sale Deed, stating that he had received a higher offer for the said property. The said refusal letter is marked as Annexure D.

11. That the Plaintiff issued a Legal Notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed as per the Agreement, but the Defendant failed to comply and maintained his refusal. The Legal Notice along with postal receipt is marked as Annexure C.

DAMAGES

12. That by reason of the wrongful refusal and breach of contract by the Defendant, the Plaintiff has suffered mental agony, financial strain, and is entitled to the costs of this suit and such other relief as this Court deems fit to grant in the interest of justice.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The present cause is governed by the Specific Relief Act, 1963, including Section 10 (mandatory after 2018 Amendment — court SHALL grant unless S.11(2), S.14, or S.16 exception), Section 16, Section 21, and Section 22.

14. The present cause is governed by the Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 10.

15. The Plaintiff also relies on the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, including Section 53A, where the pleaded facts attract those provisions.

CAUSE OF ACTION

16. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

17. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 20.06.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

18. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

19. The suit is valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty-Five Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

20. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree for specific performance of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024, directing the Defendant to execute and register the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon payment of the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/-;
    (b) In the alternative, pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 85,00,000/- towards damages for breach of contract;
    (c) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (d) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt with UTR: HDFC20240110987654

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTY

Residential flat bearing No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, bearing Survey Number {{PROPERTY_SURVEY_NUMBER}}.

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 18 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

CONSISTENCY GATE — FORBIDDEN CONTENT

The civil drafting pipeline stopped before finalization for the following reasons:
1. Draft claims 'liquidated damages s74' as relief — this is a defence concept, not a plaintiff claim.
2. Draft claims 'mitigation credit' as relief — this is a defence concept, not a plaintiff claim.

Clarify the above issues and rerun drafting.
errors: Draft claims 'liquidated damages s74' as relief — this is a defence concept, not a plaintiff claim.
